raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "The request 'What do you think?' is broad. Should I prioritize feedback on (A) the research ideas (contribution, logic, evidence) or (B) the writing and presentation (clarity, structure, polish)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The open-ended request invites feedback on the clarity and framing of the core contribution.",
    "R1.2": "Assessing the logical flow from the proposed method to the conclusions is a key part of evaluating the text.",
    "R1.3": "The request implies a check on whether the numerical results convincingly support the main claims.",
    "R1.4": "A full response requires estimating the novelty of the work relative to the state of the art.",
    "R1.6": "The general nature of the request includes evaluating the clarity, conciseness, and polish of the writing.",
    "RX.1": "The feedback must consider whether the text appropriately discloses any limitations or uncertainties.",
    "RX.2": "The feedback must identify and flag any statements that might overstate the contribution's significance or generality.",
    "RX.3": "The response must adhere to the implicit scope of providing scholarly feedback on the provided research artifact.",
    "RX.4": "The feedback should consider whether the described technology has potential for misuse or other downstream harms.",
    "RX.5": "The basis for the feedback must be transparent and directly traceable to the provided artifact and the requested responsibilities."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: The request 'What do you think?' is broad. Should I prioritize feedback on (A) the research ideas (contribution, logic, evidence) or (B) the writing and presentation (clarity, structure, polish)?

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites feedback on the clarity and framing of the core contribution.
rationale.R1.2: Assessing the logical flow from the proposed method to the conclusions is a key part of evaluating the text.
rationale.R1.3: The request implies a check on whether the numerical results convincingly support the main claims.
rationale.R1.4: A full response requires estimating the novelty of the work relative to the state of the art.
rationale.R1.6: The general nature of the request includes evaluating the clarity, conciseness, and polish of the writing.
rationale.RX.1: The feedback must consider whether the text appropriately discloses any limitations or uncertainties.
rationale.RX.2: The feedback must identify and flag any statements that might overstate the contribution's significance or generality.
rationale.RX.3: The response must adhere to the implicit scope of providing scholarly feedback on the provided research artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The feedback should consider whether the described technology has potential for misuse or other downstream harms.
rationale.RX.5: The basis for the feedback must be transparent and directly traceable to the provided artifact and the requested responsibilities.
